path: raw provider path, not OpenClaw contextWindow cap
auth_source: provider-owned OAuth profile used by OpenClaw

method.input_shape: approximate token load using repeated 'a ' content
method.success_rule: gpt-5.4 required completion confirmation near the boundary; gpt-5.4-mini and gpt-5.3-codex were treated as accepted when request creation succeeded without explicit error/context failure
method.goal: measure backend-enforced limit rather than local OpenClaw configured cap

gpt-5.4-mini.basis: request accepted unless explicit streaming error/event failure

gpt-5.3-codex.basis: request accepted unless explicit streaming error/event failure

conclusions: gpt-5.4 raw Codex backend limit is far above the current 272k local cap
gpt-5.4 raw Codex backend limit is below 1M on this path
gpt-5.4-mini and gpt-5.3-codex align closely with a 272k ceiling on the raw path
272k is not a universal openai-codex backend limit, it is model-specific on this path
